zh.Reigate St Mary Magdalene, Surrey, Engla: 英國素瑞萊蓋特 聖 瑪麗 抹大拉的
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Coles於1575年2月14日出生在英國素可普爾波洛時，她的父親George Coles30歲，母親Alice Sargent15歲。她於1591年1月1日與Edmund Freeman在英國素可普爾波洛結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1652年2月14日之前在英國素瑞萊蓋特，埋葬在英國英格蘭素瑞萊蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: Coles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): variant of Cole , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Cole
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Collis
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Collins  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Coles was born on 14 February 1575, in Pulborough, Sussex, England, her father, George Coles, was 30 and her mother, Alice Sargent, was 15. She married Edmund Freeman on 1 January 1591, in Pulborough, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died before 14 February 1652, in Reigate, Surrey, England, and was buried in Reigate, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Edmund Freeman le 1 janvier 1591, à Pulborough, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée avant 14 février 1652, à Reigate, Surrey, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Reigate, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.Reigate St Mary Magdalene, Surrey, Engla: 영국 수리 레익앳 스트 메리 막달레나
ko.lifeSummary: Alice Coles(이)가 1575년 2월 14일 영국 수세스 풀버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George Coles(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Sargent(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1591년 1월 1일 영국 수세스 풀버루그h에서 Edmund Freeman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1652년 2월 14일 이전 영국 수리 레익앳에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수리 레익앳에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Edmund Freeman em 1 de janeiro de 1591, em Pulborough, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.Reigate St Mary Magdalene, Surrey, Engla: ригэит ст мэри магдеилин, сурри, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Coles родилась 14 февраля 1575 г. в местности пулбороую, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени George Coles было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Sargent, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edmund Freeman 1 января 1591 г. в местности пулбороую, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла перед 14 февраль 1652 г. в местности ригэит, сурри, Англия и была похоронена в местности ригэит, сурри, Англия, Великобритания.
